Program_Description: The goal of the Butte County Maternal Child and Adolescent Health (MCAH) program is to ensure all children are born healthy to healthy mothers and to eliminate health status disparities.

Program_Description: WIC provides nutrition education and supplemental food to low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women, infants, and children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children
Application_Process: Visit the WIC website or contact local WIC office

Program_Description: Nurse-Family Partnership is a free home visiting program that partners registered nurses with first-time moms beginning early in their pregnancy (before 28 weeks).
Eligibility_Requirements: Beginning early in their pregnancy (before 28 weeks)

Program_Description: Nurse-Family Partnership is a free home visiting program that partners registered nurses with first-time moms beginning early in their pregnancy (before 28 weeks).
Target_Population: First-time, low-income pregnant women enrolled by 28 weeks gestation
Eligibility_Requirements: Expecting their first baby, Pregnant 28 weeks or less, Meets income requirements for Medi-Cal / WIC or under age 18, Live in Butte or Glenn County
Application_Process: Anyone can refer a woman to NFP. Women who meet eligibility requirements can refer themselves, or their prenatal and healthcare providers may refer them.
